lede: A look at June 4 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Princess Lilibet of Sussex and Oddvar Reiakvam, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Crowd collapses and crushes
events.desc: Eleven people are killed and 56 people are injured during a crowd crush incident outside M.Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru, India for the celebration of Royal Challengers Bengaluru's Indian Premier League victory.

events.title: Terry Nichols
events.desc: Terry Nichols is sentenced to life in prison for his role in the Oklahoma City bombing.

events.title: RDX
events.desc: Three cars on a train carrying hexogen to Kazakhstan explode in Arzamas, Gorky Oblast, USSR, killing 91 and injuring about 1,500.

events.title: United States Army North
events.desc: World War II: The United States Fifth Army captures Rome, although much of the German Fourteenth Army is able to withdraw to the north.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Dunkirk evacuation ends: the British Armed Forces completes evacuation of 338,000 troops from Dunkirk in France. To rally the morale of the country, Winston Churchill delivers, only to the House of Commons, his famous "We shall fight on the beaches" speech.

events.title: Pulitzer Prize
events.desc: The first Pulitzer Prizes are awarded: Laura E. Richards, Maude H. Elliott, and Florence Hall receive the first Pulitzer for biography (for Julia Ward Howe). Jean Jules Jusserand receives the first Pulitzer for history for his work With Americans of Past and Present Days. Herbert B. Swope receives the first Pulitzer for journalism for his work for the New York World.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Russia opens the Brusilov Offensive with an artillery barrage of Austro-Hungarian lines in Galicia.

events.title: Emily Davison
events.desc: Emily Davison, a suffragette, runs out in front of King George V's horse at The Derby. She is trampled, never regains consciousness, and dies four days later.

events.title: Charles Emmanuel IV
events.desc: King Charles Emmanuel IV of Sardinia abdicates his throne in favor of his brother, Victor Emmanuel.

events.title: Élisabeth Thible
events.desc: Élisabeth Thible becomes the first woman to fly in an untethered hot air balloon. Her flight covers four kilometres (2.5 mi) in 45 minutes, and reached an estimated 1,500 metres (4,900 ft) in altitude.

births.name: Karolos Papoulias
births.line: Greek lawyer and politician, 5th President of Greece (died 2021)

deaths.text: Marc Garneau, Canadian astronaut and Member of Parliament
Don Zimmer, American baseball player, coach, and manager
